name: Content Analyzer
description: Structural fact extraction from scraped content. Classifies into categories (fixed taxonomy), assigns tags, extracts key facts (people, licenses, awards, partnerships, offices, contact), and maps source citations. No summaries, opinions, or marketing prose — pure classification and extraction.
usage_notes: Pure fact extraction — haiku is sufficient and cost-effective. Increase max_content_chars for entities with extensive source material (50+ scraped pages). Runs independently of seo-planner.

options.name: ai_model
options.label: AI Model
options.description: Which model to use for content analysis. Haiku is fast and cheap for structured extraction.

options.name: reference_docs
options.label: Reference Documents
options.description: Reference docs for the analysis prompt. Key docs: master_categories.md (fixed taxonomy), master_tags.md (tag list). Upload once, reuse across runs.

options.name: temperature
options.label: Temperature
options.description: LLM temperature for analysis. Lower = more consistent extraction. Default 0.2 for reliable structured output.

options.name: max_tokens
options.label: Max output tokens
options.description: Maximum tokens in the LLM response. Increase for entities with many categories and facts.

options.name: max_content_chars
options.label: Max content characters
options.description: Truncate assembled source text to this many characters to control token usage.

options.name: prompt
options.label: Analysis prompt
options.description: The prompt sent to the AI. Use {entity_content} for scraped content, {doc:master_categories.md} and {doc:master_tags.md} for taxonomy lists, {doc:filename} for other reference docs.
